releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
Fixes the release pipeline so artifacts pass Obsidian's automated cryptographic verification.


Fixed: LightRAG server failed to start when using Gemini or Azure models with error "llm binding not supported"
Root cause: plugin was passing gemini and azure directly to LightRAG, which expects google and azure_openai respectively
releases.name: v1.4.3 — Maintenance release

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Maintenance release

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
Bug fix
LightRAG server fails to start with Gemini or Azure models ([#51](https://github.com/oscampo/obsidian-neural-composer/issues/51)): The plugin was generating LLM_BINDING=gemini and LLM_BINDING=azure, which LightRAG's API server rejects with "llm binding not supported". LightRAG expects google and azure_openai respectively. A binding name map now translates the plugin's internal provider IDs to the correct values before writing the .env file.
releases.name: v1.4.1 Bug Fix

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
**Neural Composer is now fully functional on iPhone, iPad, and Android.**

Connect to your remote LightRAG server and use every feature — chat, graph exploration, settings — from any mobile device. This is the headline change of v1.4.0, and the primary reason for the minor version bump from 1.3.6.

---

### Mobile support (iOS & Android)

Everything required to make the plugin usable on a phone or tablet, addressed end to end:

- **Settings text inputs stack vertically**: Server URL, API key, Watched folder and Exclude patterns render in column layout on non-desktop so the input field sits below its description at full width, instead of being squeezed into the narrow right slot of the default horizontal layout.
- **Settings navigation visible**: The icon nav rail and the CommandBar (Models selector and Support button) now have a 56px top offset on mobile. Obsidian's modal back button (`←`) overlays the top of the content area on mobile; without the offset, the first nav icon and the entire command bar were hidden behind it.
- **Settings focus loss fixed on mobile**: Typing in any text field (Server URL, Watched folder, etc.) no longer loses focus mid-word. All inputs now save on `blur` instead of `onChange`, so the DOM is never rebuilt while the user is typing. This also fixes the same bug on desktop (Graph & Vault tab).
- **Server-side settings hidden**: Settings that write to the local server's `.env` (Graph Logic Model, Embedding Model, Summary Language, Ontology, Reranking) are hidden on mobile and in remote mode. Those settings only make sense when the server is running locally. The Advanced tab shows a contextual explanation instead.
- **Graph toolbar icons visible on iOS**: Lucide SVGs were defaulting to 24×24px inside 18×18px button content areas and were clipped to invisible. Explicit 16×16px sizing added.
- **Graph sidebar toggle and "X" button working on iPad**: The `nrlcmp-sidebar-hidden` CSS only applied inside a `max-width: 720px` media query (phone-only). A new `min-width: 721px` rule collapses the sidebar on larger touch screens as well.
- **Depth buttons (−/+) enabled correctly**: The buttons stayed permanently disabled because their state was captured once at toolbar-creation time. The toolbar now calls `setExploreMode()` dynamically when the user switches between overview and explore mode.

---

### Other new features

- **Inline SVG rendering in chat**: Assistant responses that contain raw `<svg>...</svg>` markup now render as actual diagrams. Obsidian's `MarkdownRenderer.render()` strips raw HTML in plugin context, so SVG blocks are extracted, sanitized (script tags and `on*` event handlers removed), and injected directly into the DOM. Text portions go through the standard renderer as before.

---

### Bug fixes

- **Apply button navigation**: After accepting a diff in the Apply view, the editor now opens the modified file directly instead of leaving the user behind the diff panel.
- **Graph edge visual weight**: Edges are thinner (0.5 → 0.3 px) and use solid theme-aware colors (`#2e2e2e` dark / `#c8c8c8` light). The previous `rgba` values accumulated on the canvas — in dense graphs, dozens of overlapping semi-transparent edges summed to near-white, creating a blinding glow in dark mode.

---

### Security

npm overrides added to patch transitive dependency vulnerabilities:

| Package | Override | Advisory |
|---|---|---|
| form-data | ≥4.0.6 | GHSA-hmw2-7cc7-3qxx (CRLF injection) |
| handlebars | ≥4.7.9 | Multiple (JS injection, critical) |
| yaml | ≥2.9.0 | GHSA-48c2-rrv3-qjmp (stack overflow) |
| brace-expansion | ≥2.0.2 | GHSA-f886-m6hf-6m8v (DoS) |

**Note:** `drizzle-orm` (GHSA-gpj5-g38j-94v9, SQL injection via `escapeName()`) requires upgrading to 0.45.x, a breaking API change. The plugin uses only static schema with no user-controlled SQL identifiers, so practical risk is zero. Migration is deferred to a dedicated release.

### Acknowledgments

Thank you to the contributors who made this release possible:

- [@jagajaga](https://github.com/jagajaga) — original idea for Obsidian mobile (iOS / Android) support (PR #46).
releases.name: v1.4.0 — Full Mobile Support

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
## v1.3.6

### New features

- **LaTeX math rendering in chat** (issue #40, PR #41): Assistant messages that contain LaTeX using `\(...\)` (inline) and `\[...\]` (block) delimiters are now rendered correctly via Obsidian's MathJax engine. Previously, LLM-generated math was displayed as raw text. Inline code spans and fenced code blocks are intentionally left untouched.

- **Exclude files and folders from graph sync** (issue #42, PR #43): A new blacklist prevents specific files or subfolders inside the watched sync folder from being auto-ingested into the LightRAG graph.
  - **Settings** — a glob-pattern textarea (`Exclude patterns`) and an "Exclude hidden files and folders" toggle (enabled by default; skips
    dot-prefixed paths like `.trash` and `.obsidian`).
  - **Context menu** — right-click any file or folder inside the vault to "Exclude from graph sync". The action removes already-ingested docs for those paths from the graph and toggles to "Re-include in graph sync".
  - Every auto-ingestion entry point (file create, modify, rename, and batch folder ingest) respects the exclusion rules before showing any processing
    state.

- **Scalable folder removal from the graph** (PR #39): Removing a folder from the LightRAG graph now works correctly on large vaults.
  - Full pagination — all documents are resolved across every page of  `/documents/paginated` (previously capped at the first 200).
  - Batched deletions — doc IDs are sent in chunks of 100 to  `/documents/delete_document` instead of one request per file, dramatically
    reducing round-trips.
  - Live progress — the same pipeline monitor used during ingestion now surfaces the server-side deletion job name, percentage, and status message until the operation completes.

### Internal / developer-facing

- **ESLint 9 flat config with enforced CI** (PR #45): Migrated from `.eslintrc.js`  to `eslint.config.mjs` with `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd/recommended`. CI now  runs `npx eslint . --max-warnings 0` with no `continue-on-error`, so contributor PRs fail automatically without needing a manual Obsidian bot review cycle (I hope). Unified on `typescript-eslint@^8`; removed conflicting v5
  packages and the `ajv` override.
- Resolved all lint violations surfaced by the stricter config across ~20 source files (sentence-case strings, `no-console`, unused variables, import ordering).

### Acknowledgments

Thank you to the contributors who made this release possible:

- [@niveKKumar](https://github.com/niveKKumar) — first-time contributor.
  Reported and implemented the LaTeX math rendering fix (#40 / PR #41) and the graph sync exclusion feature (#42 / PR #43).

- [@jagajaga](https://github.com/jagajaga) — scalable folder removal from the graph (PR #39): full pagination support, batched deletions, and live progress monitoring. Code from this PR was incorporated into the exclude-from-sync implementation.
releases.name: v1.3.6 LaTeX Math Rendering, Graph Sync Exclusions & Scalable Folder Removal

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
v1.3.5 Security Patch (2026-06-03)

Security

  • Dependency vulnerability overrides The Obsidian plugin review system (updated May 13, 2026) flagged 36 vulnerability warnings in v1.3.4, dropping the plugin scorecard from 89 to 79 points. Investigation confirmed that none of the vulnerabilities were introduced by v1.3.4, the CVE database was updated after v1.3.3 shipped, retroactively flagging packages that were already present. This release applies npm overrides to force patched versions of 10 transitive dependencies, reducing production warnings from 36 to 1:

    Package | Fix -- | -- hono | >=4.12.18 , 14 warnings eliminated @hono/node-server | >=2.0.0, 2 warnings eliminated @xmldom/xmldom | >=0.9.4, 5 warnings eliminated lodash-es | >=4.18.1, 2 warnings eliminated qs | >=6.15.2, 2 warnings eliminated path-to-regexp | >=8.4.0, 2 warnings eliminated fast-uri | >=3.1.2, 2 warnings eliminated ajv | >=8.17.2, 1 warning eliminated express-rate-limit | >=8.5.1, 1 warning eliminated ip-address | >=10.2.0, 1 warning eliminated

  • uuid upgraded ^10.0.0 → ^11.1.1, fixes GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq (missing buffer bounds check). Direct dependency update, no breaking change in plugin usage.

  • langchain removed, Replaced RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter with a lightweight custom implementation (src/utils/text-splitter.ts) that replicates identical behavior. Removes 22 packages and all associated vulnerability warnings from the dependency tree.

Note: drizzle-orm (GHSA-gpj5-g38j-94v9, SQL injection in improperly escaped identifiers) remains at 0.39.x. The fix requires migrating to 0.45.x, a breaking API change that will be addressed in a separate release with full regression testing on the vector database. The vulnerability does not affect this plugin in practice, as all SQL inputs come from the user's own local vault files.
releases.name: v1.3.5  Security Patch (2026-06-03)

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
### v1.3.4  (2026-06-02)

**Bug fixes**

- **Fix: LightRAG remote authentication**  The plugin was sending the API key as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`, which LightRAG does not recognize. All requests now use the correct `X-API-Key: <key>` header. Users connecting to a remote LightRAG server secured with `LIGHTRAG_API_KEY` no longer receive `401 Invalid token` errors. Closes #37. (Thanks to [GeorgelPreput](https://github.com/GeorgelPreput) for the detailed bug report and fix — PR #38)

- **Fix: LM Studio and other local providers fail to ingest without an API key**  LightRAG's Python client accesses `OPENAI_API_KEY` directly via `os.environ[]` and raises `KeyError` when it is absent, even when using a non-OpenAI base URL. The plugin now writes `OPENAI_API_KEY=no-api-key` as a placeholder in the generated `.env` when no real OpenAI key is configured. The placeholder satisfies the client initializer without affecting authentication. Closes #34. (Thanks to [rb3rg3r-x](https://github.com/rb3rg3r-x)  for the report)

**New features**

- **Folder context menu: Ingest XOR Remove from graph** — Right-clicking a folder now shows only the relevant action: _"Ingest folder into graph"_ if the folder has no documents in the graph, or _"Remove folder from graph"_ if it does. The new Remove action opens a confirmation modal, shows per-file progress, and updates the file-explorer status dots for files inside the Watched folder. Both items are shown as a fallback when the server is unreachable at startup. (Thanks to [jagajaga](https://github.com/jagajaga), PR #35)

**UI improvements**

- **CommandBar redesign** The three model-selector pills (Chat / Apply / Embed) are now collapsed into a single **Models ▾** button. Clicking it opens a compact dropdown showing all three active models; selecting any one navigates directly to the Models tab. The **Support** button remains right-aligned and always visible. The header now works correctly at any window width, including the smaller resizable settings window introduced in Obsidian 1.13.0, and is ready for mobile.
releases.name: v1.3.4 — Remote Auth Fix, Folder Graph Management & CommandBar Redesign

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description: This completes the fix that was missing from the PR #31
releases.name: v1.3.3 This completes the fix that was missing from the PR #31

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
Fixed a critical bug in src/core/llm/gemini.ts where Gemini 2.5+ thinking models fail when tools are enabled:

- Root cause: Gemini's thinking models require a thought_signature field on every FunctionCallPart when tools are in use. The current SDK (@google/generative-ai v0.24.x) does not propagate this field, causing 400 errors on tool calls.
- Solution: When tools are present in the request, set thinkingBudget: 0 to disable thinking mode for that request. This removes the thought_signature requirement while preserving tool functionality.
- Future: Added TODO comment to migrate to @google/genai SDK v2+ which has full thought_signature support, allowing thinking and tools to coexist.
releases.name: v1.3.2 Fix: Gemini Thinking Mode with Tools

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
v1.3.1 — Minor bug fixes
CSS compatibility fix
	•	Removed all !important declarations from styles.css — replaced with higher-specificity selectors to comply with the Obsidian plugin linter guidelines. No visual changes.
releases.name: v1.3.1 — Minor bug fixes

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
### ✨ New Features

**Document status indicators in the file explorer**
Files inside the watched folder now display a colored dot next to their name showing their LightRAG ingestion status:
- 🟢 Processed — in the knowledge graph and up to date
- 🟡 Processing — being ingested (dot pulses)
- 🔴 Failed — ingestion error
- 🔵 Removed — intentionally excluded from the graph

**Watched folder aggregate indicator**
The watched folder itself shows a single dot summarizing the state of all its documents — green when everything is processed, yellow if anything needs attention, blue if some docs have been removed.

---

### 🛠 Improvements

**Settings panel redesign**
The settings page has a new layout: a compact icon rail on the left lets you switch between sections (Providers, Models, Chat, Graph & Vault, Tools, Advanced, Help) without scrolling. A command bar at the top shows the active Chat / Apply / Embed models at a glance.

**Smarter server sync**
On startup, the plugin reconciles the local status cache against the LightRAG server and corrects any stale entries. A pipeline watcher polls the server every 2 seconds during ingestion and updates statuses automatically when processing finishes.

**"Reprocess folder" now recovers stuck documents**
Documents stuck in *Processing* state from a previous interrupted session are included in folder reprocessing, not skipped.
releases.name: v1.3.0 Settings UI redesign, doc status system & LightRAG version detection

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
## v1.2.3 Provider Config Fixes & Server Reliability (contributed by [@nicholas-leonard](https://github.com/nicholas-leonard) )

### 🐛 Bug Fix: OpenRouter & Custom Providers Return 401

When a provider record had no explicit `baseUrl` stored (common for accounts created before that field was required), `generateEnvConfig` omitted `LLM_BINDING_HOST` entirely. LightRAG then silently fell back to `api.openai.com`, rejecting non-OpenAI API keys with a 401 error and no clear indication of the cause.

The plugin now includes a fallback base-URL map for well-known providers. If no `baseUrl` is stored for a given provider, the correct endpoint is resolved automatically:

| Provider | Resolved URL |
|---|---|
| OpenRouter | `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` |
| Groq | `https://api.groq.com/openai/v1` |
| Deepseek | `https://api.deepseek.com` |
| Mistral | `https://api.mistral.ai/v1` |
| Perplexity | `https://api.perplexity.ai` |
| LM Studio | `http://localhost:1234/v1` |

The same fallback logic applies to the embedding binding, so mixed-provider setups (e.g. OpenRouter for LLM + a custom embedding provider) are resolved correctly and independently.

---

### 🐛 Bug Fix: Ollama Embedding Returns 404 When Used With a Remote LLM

LightRAG has a bug in its `get_default_host('ollama')` function — it reads `LLM_BINDING_HOST` as a fallback instead of using a proper Ollama default. When the LLM was pointed at a remote provider (e.g. OpenRouter), the Ollama embedding client was silently routed to that remote host and received 404 errors on every embedding call.

The plugin now always writes `EMBEDDING_BINDING_HOST=http://localhost:11434` explicitly for native Ollama, bypassing the faulty fallback entirely. Additionally, Ollama's `/v1` suffix is no longer appended — Ollama's embedding endpoint follows its own `/api/*` path convention, not the OpenAI-compatible `/v1/*` schema.

---

### ✨ New: Reprocess Failed Documents

A new **"Reprocess failed"** button is available in Settings → Neural Backend. After fixing a broken LLM configuration (wrong API key, missing base URL, etc.), documents that failed during entity extraction remain in a `failed` state and are not automatically retried. This button re-submits them to LightRAG's `/documents/reprocess_failed` endpoint without requiring a full vault re-ingestion.

---

### 🔧 Improvement: Orphaned Server Process Cleanup (macOS / Linux)

`stopLightRagServer` previously only handled Windows via `taskkill`. On macOS and Linux, if LightRAG was started outside the plugin (manually or from a previous Obsidian session), restarting from the plugin had no effect — the old process kept the port occupied.

The plugin now uses `lsof` to find and terminate any process listening on the configured server port before starting a new one, so plugin-initiated restarts reliably take effect regardless of how the previous server was started.
releases.name: v1.2.3 Provider Config Fixes & Server Reliability

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
### Bug Fix: Active Embedding Model Has No UI Control (contributed by [@nicholas-leonard](https://github.com/nicholas-leonard) )

Users relying on non-OpenAI embedding providers (Ollama, Gemini, and others) were silently broken after v1.1.x: the dropdown to select the **active embedding model** was inadvertently removed when `RAGSection` was hidden as LightRAG became the primary RAG backend.

The `embeddingModelId` setting was still being read at runtime by `RAGEngine` for every local vector lookup — but with no way to change it from the UI, it stayed locked to its default value (`openai/text-embedding-3-small`). Any user without an OpenAI key received silent authentication errors on every smart context search with no clear indication of the cause.

**Fix:** An **"Active embedding model"** dropdown has been added to Settings → Models → Embedding Models, directly above the models table. This is the natural home for the control — users already manage which models are available there, so selecting the active one belongs in the same place. Models marked as recommended display a `(Recommended)` badge, consistent with the rest of the settings UI.

No behaviour changes — this restores a missing UI control for a setting that was already being persisted and used at runtime.
releases.name: v1.2.2 — Embedding Model Selector Restored

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
### 🗺️ Graph View: Two-Mode Architecture

The graph view now operates in two distinct modes that can be switched at any time:

- **Overview mode** (default on load) — renders every node in the graph regardless of connectivity. Fetches all labels via `/graph/label/list` and displays isolated and disconnected nodes alongside the main graph. The `+`/`−` depth buttons are disabled in this mode (depth is irrelevant when showing all nodes).
- **Explore mode** — triggered by searching for an entity or clicking any node. Renders the subgraph reachable from that entity up to a user-controlled depth (1–10 levels, adjusted with the `+`/`−` buttons). The stats bar shows the current depth and node/edge count.

Reloading the graph always returns to Overview mode.

---

### 🔍 Graph View: Isolated & Orphan Nodes

Previously, nodes with no edges were invisible in the graph — only reachable nodes were fetched via BFS traversal. This release adds a second pass in Overview mode that augments the graph with every label returned by `/graph/label/list`, making disconnected components visible. Node Manager classifies them:

- **Unexplored** — present in the label index but not yet traversed (shown in italics)
- **Orphan** — in the graph store with degree 0 (shown with accent color)

---

### 👆 Graph View: Single-Click on Isolated Nodes

Clicking an isolated (placeholder) node previously required three steps: click to open empty panel → "Explore from here" → click node again. Now a single click auto-triggers exploration from that node and immediately shows its full detail panel — no extra clicks needed.

---

### 🔗 Graph View: Context Sources Fixed

The node detail panel was showing "Unknown" for all source documents regardless of the node selected. Root cause: `kv_store_doc_status.json` uses the vault-relative file path as its JSON key, but the lookup was using the embedded document ID. Fixed by dual-indexing the map with both the key and the embedded `id` field, with six fallback paths (`file_name`, `file_path`, `_rawKey`, etc.) to cover both current and older LightRAG storage formats.

---

### 📊 RAG Engine: Real Relevance Scores

Reference documents in the "Context used" panel previously showed a hardcoded placeholder score. Scores are now computed from citation frequency: LightRAG embeds `[1]`, `[2]` … markers in its response when `include_references=true`. The plugin counts how often each source is cited in the answer and normalizes the counts into a 0–1 relevance score:

- Cited ≥ 1 time → `0.40 + (citations / max) × 0.55` (range 0.40–0.95)
- Listed but never cited → `0.20`

Scores are color-coded green (≥ 80 %), yellow (≥ 40 %), or muted (< 40 %).

---

### 💬 Chat: Context Used Panel

- **"Graph's memory" removed** — the master LightRAG answer was always listed at 100% relevance and carried no information the user couldn't already see in the response. It is now filtered out; the badge count reflects only real source documents.
- **Score/filename spacing fixed** — the percentage value was visually colliding with the document name (e.g. `95%2506.02404v3.pdf`). Layout now uses inline pixel margins that are theme-independent, preventing the collision regardless of which Obsidian theme is active.

---

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **Stray `<nrlcmp_block>` tags with citations off** — a case-sensitivity mismatch (`"Graph's Memory"` vs `"Graph's memory"`) meant the graph context was never matched as special content and was passed to the LLM as a plain code block keyed by filename. The model then generated empty `<nrlcmp_block filename="Graph's memory" startLine="…">` reference tags that appeared as raw HTML in the chat. Fixed by correcting the match and, when "Show citations in chat" is disabled, stripping `[N]` markers from the context before it reaches the LLM.

- **Nested `<nrlcmp_block>` references not rendering** — when the LLM embeds inline file references (`<nrlcmp_block filename="Notes/..." startLine="N" endLine="M">`) inside its markdown response block, they were previously passed as raw strings to `ObsidianMarkdown` and rendered as visible HTML text. The markdown preview renderer in `MarkdownCodeComponent` now runs `parseTagContents()` recursively on the block content, so inline references are displayed as proper expandable `MarkdownReferenceBlock` components. References to non-vault paths (e.g. internal graph context labels) render nothing instead of showing raw tags.

---
releases.name: v1.2.1 — Graph Intelligence & Chat Polish

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
### ✨ New Feature: Incremental Vault Sync (Issue #2)

The most requested feature. Define a **Watched Folder** in Settings → Neural Backend → Vault Sync, and Neural Composer will automatically keep your LightRAG graph in sync:

- **Create** a file in the folder → ingested automatically (2 s delay to let the OS finish writing)
- **Delete** a file → removed from the graph index instantly
- **Rename or move** a file → old entry removed, new one ingested
- **Modify** a file → re-indexed automatically (5 s debounce to avoid noise during editing)

All events show a persistent in-app notification that updates as the operation completes, so you always know what's happening with your graph.

Supports `.md`, `.txt`, `.csv`, `.json`, `.html`, `.xml` as text documents, and any other extension as a binary upload.

---

### 🎨 2D Graph Visual Polish

- **Theme-aware labels** — node names now use a light color in dark mode and a dark color in light mode. Previously they were always white, making them invisible in light themes.
- **Theme-aware canvas background** — the graph background adapts automatically (dark grey in dark mode, soft grey in light mode).
- **Hover label bubble** — Sigma's hardcoded white hover bubble is now replaced by a custom renderer: near-black in dark mode, white in light mode. Labels are always readable on hover and on click.
- **Selected node** — in light mode the selected node now renders in cyan (`#00d4ff`) instead of white, which was invisible against the light background.

---

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **Graph sidebar layout** — the Node Manager panel now appears correctly to the right of the graph, not below it (CSS selector specificity fix).
- **Vault sync document lookup** — the LightRAG `/documents/paginated` response uses the key `documents`, not `statuses`. This fix ensures delete and rename operations actually work.
- **Document identity** — files are now tracked by their vault-relative path (`Folder/Note.md`) instead of bare filename (`Note.md`), preventing collisions between files with the same name in different folders. Backward-compatible fallback for documents indexed with older versions.

---

### ⚙️ Internal

- Settings schema migrated to **version 14** (`lightRagAutoSync` boolean removed, `lightRagSyncFolder` string added).
releases.name: v1.2.0 — Incremental Vault Sync & Graph Visual Polish

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
## What’s changed
This release fixes two bugs that were reported in issue #17 before v1.1.14 but were inadvertently left out of that fix, apologies to the users who reported them.
## Bug fixes
- Manual .env edits no longer overwritten on restart, saveEnvAndRestart() was writing the user’s manually-edited content to .env and then triggering restartLightRagServer(), which after a 2-second delay called updateEnvFile() and silently replaced the file with auto-generated content. The restart flow now preserves whatever is on disk when triggered from the manual editor. (Thanks @GitMorRic)
- Binding host URLs are now normalized to include /v1, LLM_BINDING_HOST and EMBEDDING_BINDING_HOST were written to .env using the raw provider URL as stored, which may or may not include the /v1 path segment. LightRAG’s Python OpenAI client uses base_url directly and requires /v1 to be present. The generator now strips any trailing slashes and consistently appends /v1 for all OpenAI-compatible bindings (OLLAMA_HOST is unaffected). (Thanks @Pub-Craig-Researchs)


## No action required
If you already have a working setup, no settings changes are needed. Clicking Apply changes & restart in Neural settings will regenerate the .env with the corrected URLs.
releases.name: v1.1.16: hotfix release

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
## Performance
main.js reduced from 8.9 MB to 3.1 MB (65% smaller)
This release is entirely focused on bundle efficiency. The main.js > 5 MB Obsidian scorecard warning is resolved — users on the Obsidian Sync Standard plan can now sync the plugin file without issues.
Four independent optimizations were applied:
- Replaced js-tiktoken with a lightweight approximation
js-tiktoken (5.35 MB of BPE tokenizer data) was used only to estimate whether a chunk of text exceeded a context-window threshold. A simple character-based approximation (chars / 4) is equally effective for this use case and adds zero bytes to the bundle.
- Switched syntax highlighter to the light build
react-syntax-highlighter was bundling all ~200 Prism language definitions via refractor. Switched to the PrismLight build with only the 10 languages actually used in the chat view (TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Bash, JSON, CSS, SQL, YAML, Rust, Markdown).
- Fixed lucide-react icon tree-shaking
The icon library was bundling all 1,537 icons despite only ~37 being used. Implemented an esbuild plugin that redirects lucide-react barrel imports to a curated file that imports each icon from its individual ESM path — only the icons actually used are now included.
- Lazy-loaded 3D force graph
3d-force-graph (which pulls in three.js) is now loaded via dynamic import() only when the 3D graph view is first rendered, keeping it out of the static module graph.

## Documentation
Added a Privacy & Security section to the README that documents every network call the plugin makes, explains each Obsidian scorecard disclosure in plain English, and clarifies that no telemetry, analytics, or unexpected external requests are made.
releases.name: v1.1.15 — Performance: Bundle Size Reduction

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
Bug Fixes
Issue [#18](https://github.com/oscampo/obsidian-neural-composer/issues/18) — LM Studio (127.0.0.1) not connecting to LightRAG
The .env generator was only writing LLM_BINDING_HOST when the base URL contained localhost. Addresses like 127.0.0.1 (LM Studio's default) were silently ignored, causing LightRAG to fall back to the real OpenAI API and fail with 401. Fixed to write the custom URL for any non-default base URL.

Issue [#17](https://github.com/oscampo/obsidian-neural-composer/issues/17) — Custom embedding models not appearing in Graph Embedding Model dropdown
Two fixes:

The Graph Embedding Model dropdown now updates in real time when a new embedding model is added — no need to close and reopen the settings tab.
generateEnvConfig() now uses LightRAG's dedicated LLM_BINDING_HOST / LLM_BINDING_API_KEY and EMBEDDING_BINDING_HOST / EMBEDDING_BINDING_API_KEY variables instead of the shared OPENAI_BASE_URL / OPENAI_API_KEY. This allows configuring different providers for LLM and embedding independently without one overwriting the other.
Issue [#16](https://github.com/oscampo/obsidian-neural-composer/issues/16) — "Folder not found" on Windows for Ontology Source Folder
The field now shows a live autocomplete dropdown of vault folders as you type, instead of a free-text input. This prevents the error caused by entering absolute filesystem paths — the field now always produces the vault-relative path that LightRAG expects.

Scorecard & Code Quality (Obsidian Community)
Removed all 45 !important declarations from styles.css using proper CSS specificity techniques (ID scoping, compound selectors, parent scoping).
Removed eslint-plugin-react from dev dependencies (flagged by scorecard).
Merged duplicate .nrlcmp-textarea CSS selector.
Fixed CI Prettier check step (invalid --cache-strategy flag).
Updated settings.test.ts to include lightRag* default fields.
releases.name: v1.1.14 — Bug Fixes & UX Improvements

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
With the launch of the new Obsidian Community platform and its automated plugin health checks, Neural Composer received a scorecard review. This release addresses the warnings flagged by that system to keep the plugin in good standing with the community directory.

## Code Quality Fixes
	•	Removed lodash dependencies: lodash.debounce and lodash.isequal have been replaced with lean native implementations, eliminating two flagged package dependencies and reducing bundle overhead.
	•	Removed builtin-modules package: Now uses Node.js’s own builtinModules from the standard node:module API instead of the third-party package.
	•	Replaced eslint-plugin-import: Migrated to eslint-plugin-i, the actively maintained fork recommended as the modern replacement.

## CSS Lint Fixes
	•	Removed duplicate CSS properties across multiple rules (font-size, padding, background-color, border, align-items, and others).
	•	Merged duplicate selectors (.nrlcmp-popover ul, .nrlcmp-popover li:hover, .nrlcmp-toolbar-input:focus, and several sidebar component classes).
	•	Expanded all shorthand hex colors to full 6-digit format (#fff → #ffffff, etc.).
	•	Replaced column-gap + row-gap with the gap shorthand to fix the multicolumn browser support flag.

## Build & Release Pipeline
	•	Artifact Attestations: The release workflow now generates GitHub artifact attestations for both main.js and styles.css via actions/attest-build-provenance. This establishes cryptographic provenance for every build, letting users verify that release assets were produced directly from this repository.
	•	Updated GitHub Actions to latest versions (checkout@v4, setup-node@v4, create-pull-request@v6) and switched to npm ci for reproducible CI installs.

## Meta
	•	Updated fundingUrl to Ko-fi. If Neural Composer has been useful to you, a coffee is always appreciated! ☕
releases.name: v1.1.13 - Community Health: Scorecard Fixes & Build Pipeline

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description: Due to new policies in Obsidian Community , new checks are needed to pass as an approved plugin. This is a little change in this way
releases.name: v1.1.12 - Last minute Hotfix: minAppVersion is 1.7.2

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This is a critical security hotfix addressing a lingering hardcoded configuration.

### Security Fixes
*   **Total Localhost Enforcement:** While v1.1.8 updated the auto-generated `.env` file, a secondary hardcoded host setting was discovered in the core server initialization logic (line 454 of `main.ts`). This release permanently switches that value from `0.0.0.0` to `127.0.0.1`.
*   **Result:** Your local LightRAG server is now fully locked down and will no longer listen on public network interfaces, protecting your knowledge base from external access on shared networks.

### Community Recognition
A special thanks to **@spark706** for their exceptional diligence in reviewing the source code and identifying the exact line where this oversight was located. Your contribution is vital to the security of this project! 

---
**Update Instructions:**
As this affects the server launch logic, we recommend:
1. Updating to **v1.1.11**.
2. Clicking the **"Restart Server Now"** button in settings to ensure the new host configuration is active.
releases.name: v1.1.11 - Security Hotfix: Complete Localhost Lockdown

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This maintenance release focuses on squashing legacy bugs in the settings menu and fixing connection mapping for custom embedding providers. 

### Bug Fixes

*   **Custom Embedding Provider Mapping (Fix for #13):** 
    Applied the "Smart Mapping" logic to Embedding models. Previously, custom embedding providers (like SiliconFlow or local instances) would incorrectly write their raw ID to the `.env` file, causing LightRAG to fail. The plugin now intelligently routes custom embedding providers to use the OpenAI-compatible protocol (`EMBEDDING_BINDING=openai`), properly injecting your custom Base URL and API Key.
*   **Settings Deletion Crash (Fix for #11):** 
    Fixed a silent crash that occurred when clicking the "Trash" icon to delete a Custom Provider or Model in the settings. This was caused by a legacy check looking for a local vector database (`getVectorManager is not a function`) that was removed when we migrated to the LightRAG backend. Deleting providers is now instant and error-free.

### Community Credits
Massive thanks to **@1997marss** and **@joshwapohlmann** for diving into the console logs, providing stack traces, and making debugging these edge cases a breeze!
releases.name: v1.1.10 - Custom Providers & Settings Cleanup

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This is a monumental release for Neural Composer! We are incredibly proud to integrate our very first major community-driven feature, transforming the plugin from a strictly local tool into a fully distributed Client-Server architecture.

###  New Features

*   ** Remote Server Support:** 
    You are no longer restricted to running the LightRAG backend on the same machine as Obsidian. You can now host your heavy LightRAG engine on a dedicated GPU server (or a cloud instance) and connect your Obsidian vault to it from your lightweight laptop.
*   ** Secure Authentication:** 
    Added support for Bearer Token Authentication. You can now securely connect to your remote LightRAG instances across the network or via tunnels (like Tailscale) without leaving your endpoints unprotected.
*   ** Smart Resource Management:** 
    The plugin intelligently recognizes when you are using a Remote Server and cleanly disables the local server auto-start mechanisms, saving your local machine's CPU and RAM.
*   ** Seamless Settings Migration:** 
    Settings have been updated to cleanly separate Local and Remote configurations without breaking existing local setups.

###  Community Spotlight
A massive, heartfelt thank you to **@marsch (Mario Scheliga)** for conceptualizing, writing, and submitting this feature in PR #15! The code quality, inclusion of tests, and adherence to Obsidian's strict guidelines were impeccable. Thank you for helping Neural Composer level up! 

---

###  How to use the new Remote Feature
1. Go to **Settings > Neural Composer > Neural Backend**.
2. Toggle the connection mode from Local to **Remote**.
3. Enter your remote server URL (e.g., `http://192.168.1.100:9621`) and your authentication token.
4. Reload the plugin!
releases.name: v1.1.9 - The Remote Brain (Community Edition!)

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This is a swift security patch to ensure the privacy of your local Knowledge Graph.

###  Security Fixes
*   **Localhost Binding:** Changed the auto-generated LightRAG server host from `0.0.0.0` to `127.0.0.1`. Previously, the server was listening on all network interfaces, which could potentially expose your local RAG API to other devices on your local network (e.g., public Wi-Fi). The server is now strictly locked down to your local machine.

###  Community Credits
A massive thank you to **@Spark706** on the Obsidian forums for catching this security oversight and conducting a sharp review of the network behavior!
releases.name: v1.1.8 - Security Patch: Localhost Binding

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This update focuses on crucial backend stability, resolving issues that prevented the LightRAG engine from spawning correctly in certain local environments, while also aligning our UI with Obsidian's strict official guidelines.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

*   **Windows Path Sanitization (Fix for #7  ):** 
    Resolved a critical issue where the LightRAG backend would crash upon initialization on Windows if the Obsidian Vault or the Python execution path contained spaces (e.g., `C:\Users\My Name\Obsidian Vault`). The plugin now securely parses and wraps all command execution paths, preventing shell argument fragmentation.
*   **Custom Provider Mapping (Fix for #8):** 
    Corrected a logic flaw in `.env` generation. Previously, defining a custom LLM provider would incorrectly write the provider's arbitrary ID directly as the `LLM_BINDING`. The plugin now intelligently routes all Custom Providers to use the standard `openai` binding protocol while preserving your custom Host URL and API Key. This resolves backend crashes and subsequent CORS errors when connecting to local servers like LM Studio or vLLM.

### 🛠️ Under the Hood (Compliance)

*   **Linter & UI Standards:** Executed a comprehensive pass over the codebase to ensure strict adherence to Obsidian's official plugin guidelines. All UI text has been converted to sentence case (e.g., "Graph Data Directory" -> "Graph data directory"), and redundant async operations have been cleaned up. This prepares Neural Composer for its final official review.

### 🤝 Community Credits
A huge thank you to **@mattie-24g** and **@miqsoft** for their incredibly detailed bug reports and forensic analysis on GitHub. Your feedback directly shaped this release!
releases.name: v1.1.7 - Core Stability & System Compatibility

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This release marks a major milestone in **Neural Composer's** journey toward becoming a complete Knowledge Management Suite. We've introduced powerful tools for graph curation and enhanced system transparency.

### 🕸️ New Feature: The Relationship Weaver
You are no longer a passive observer of your graph. You are now its architect.
*   **Manual Linking:** Select two or more nodes in the **Node Manager** and use the new **"Link"** tool to create explicit connections between them.
*   **🪄 AI-Powered Suggestions:** Within the connection modal, use the "Suggest with AI" button. The plugin will query your **currently configured LLM** (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, or local via Ollama) to analyze both entities and suggest a logical, technical description for their relationship.
*   **Graph Evolution:** These manual links are injected directly into the LightRAG engine, updating your Knowledge Graph and improving future retrieval accuracy for those concepts.

### 🚥 New Feature: Real-time System Status
No more guessing if the backend is alive.
*   **Status Bar Indicator:** A new, minimalist indicator in the Obsidian status bar shows the real-time health of the LightRAG server.
    *   🟢 **Online:** System ready.
    *   🟡 **Busy:** Ingesting data or thinking.
    *   🔴 **Offline:** Connection lost.
*   **Reactive Heartbeat:** The indicator uses a smart "heartbeat" logic to detect server status changes instantly. 
*   **One-Click Recovery:** If the server is offline, simply click the status indicator to trigger a restart attempt directly.

### 🎒 Portable & Zero-Config Architecture
*   **Automatic Workspace:** New users no longer need to manually create data folders. The plugin now automatically configures a portable, hidden folder (`.neural_memory`) inside your Vault.
*   **Universal Portability:** Your graph data now travels with your vault files, making it easier than ever to sync between devices via Git or cloud services.

### 🛠️ Stability & Refinements
*   **3D Engine Safety:** Implemented advanced edge-filtering to prevent "Dangling Node" crashes in large vaults.
*   **2D Navigation Overhaul:** Fixed a "Warp Drive" coordinate bug. The camera now travels to absolute coordinates, ensuring the target node is perfectly centered, even if it was off-screen.
*   **Security Hardening:** Completed a full refactor of UI components to eliminate `innerHTML` usage and isolate CSS classes (`.nrlcmp-`), strictly adhering to Obsidian's official security and compatibility guidelines.
releases.name: v1.1.6 - The Relationship Weaver & System Vitality

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This is a **critical update** for users relying on Google Gemini for embeddings.

### 🐛 The Fix
Google officially deprecated the `text-embedding-004` model on **January 14, 2026**. This caused ingestion failures for new users or those resetting their configuration.
*   **Updated Defaults:** Replaced the deprecated model with the current stable standard: **`gemini-embedding-001`** (768 dimensions).
*   **Settings UI:** The dropdown menu now correctly lists the available Gemini models.

### ⚠️ Important Migration Note
If you are switching from the old `004` model to the new `001` model:
1.  Go to **Settings > Neural Composer > Neural Backend**.
2.  Select **`gemini - gemini-embedding-001`** in the "Graph Embedding Model" dropdown.
3.  Click **"Restart Server Now"**.
4.  **Action Required:** You must **Delete your Graph Data folder** (or the `.neural_memory` folder) and **Re-Ingest your notes**.
    *   *Why?* Vectors generated by different models are mathematically incompatible. If you don't re-ingest, semantic search will fail.

### 🤝 Credits
Special thanks to **@apshoemaker** for flagging this issue promptly!
releases.name: v1.1.5 - Critical Hotfix: Gemini Embeddings Update

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
**This is the Release Candidate for the official Obsidian Community Plugins submission.**
It focuses on code hygiene, security compliance, and visual stability, ensuring a conflict-free experience alongside other plugins.

### 🛡️ Security & Architecture
*   **DOM Sanitization:** Refactored all UI components (Graph Manager, Modals) to eliminate `innerHTML` usage, strictly adhering to Obsidian's security guidelines against XSS.
*   **CSS Isolation:** Renamed and scoped all generic CSS classes (e.g., textareas) to the `.nrlcmp-` namespace, preventing style bleeding or conflicts when installed alongside similar plugins (like Smart Composer).
*   **Path Safety:** Improved file path resolution logic to handle cross-platform consistency.

### 🕸️ Native Graph Manager
*   **Fixed 2D Navigation:** Resolved a coordinate synchronization issue where the camera would travel to the wrong location when searching for off-screen nodes. The "Search & Fly" feature now uses absolute graph coordinates for pinpoint accuracy.
*   **Source Resolution:** Fixed the "Unknown Source" bug. The node details panel now robustly extracts and links to the correct source files (`.md`, `.pdf`) from the LightRAG metadata.
*   **Visual Logic:** Standardized label visibility logic. In 2D mode, labels are hidden to reduce noise and appear in high contrast upon interaction.

### 🎛️ Performance
*   **Console Hygiene:** Removed verbose logging to keep the developer console clean for users.
*   **Dependency Cleanup:** Removed unused graph layout libraries to reduce plugin footprint.

> **Update Note:** Please **restart Obsidian** after updating to ensure the new styles and scripts load correctly.
releases.name: v1.1.4 - Security Hardening & Graph Polish

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This release introduces advanced configuration tools for power users, giving you granular control over the Neural Backend performance, and significantly improves error detection for a smoother experience.

### 🎛️ Advanced Configuration & Tuning

*   **Performance Tuning UI:** Added a dedicated section in settings to adjust critical performance parameters directly:
    *   `MAX_ASYNC`: Control concurrency to prevent Rate Limits (429) on free tiers (e.g., Gemini).
    *   `MAX_PARALLEL_INSERT`: Throttle ingestion speed for stability on older hardware or HDDs.
*   **Custom Environment Editor:** New "Advanced Configuration" block allows you to inject raw environment variables into the `.env` file. These overrides persist and take precedence over default settings.
*   **Template Loader:** Added a one-click "Insert Template" button to load all available LightRAG configuration options for easy reference and tuning.

### 🛡️ Error Handling & UX

*   **Smart Error Detection:** The plugin now intelligently distinguishes between **Provider Rate Limits** (e.g., Gemini/OpenAI 429) and **Reranker Quotas**, giving specific advice on how to fix each (e.g., "Reduce MAX_ASYNC" vs "Check Rerank Credits").
*   **Overload Protection:** Added handling for `503 Service Unavailable` errors to inform the user when the LLM provider is temporarily overloaded, rather than reporting invalid credentials.
*   **Gemini Compatibility:** Improved default settings handling to prevent configuration conflicts when switching between OpenAI and Gemini providers.

> **Update Note:** Please **restart Obsidian** after updating to ensure the new configuration logic takes effect.
releases.name: v1.1.3 - Total Control & Smart Error Handling

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
**This release focuses on "Zero-Friction" usability and visual stability.** We've automated the data storage setup and refined the Native Graph engine to handle large vaults beautifully.

### ✨ New Features

*   **🎒 Portable Knowledge Graph (Zero-Config):**
    *   **Automatic Setup:** If no data directory is defined, the plugin now automatically creates a hidden folder (`.neural_memory`) **inside your Vault**.
    *   **Portability:** Your graph now travels with your vault. If you move your vault to another computer or sync it via Git, your AI memory goes with you effortlessly.
    *   **Performance:** The folder starts with `.` to prevent Obsidian from indexing thousands of internal JSON files, keeping your vault fast.

*   **🎨 Visual Engine Overhaul (2D):**
    *   **Smart Labels:** Implemented a "Noise Reduction" logic. Only major nodes (hubs) show labels by default to keep the view clean.
    *   **Improved Readability:** Tuned label colors (Blue-Violet) and visibility thresholds to ensure text remains legible against the dark background without overcrowding the graph.
    *   **Physics Tuning:** Adjusted the `fcose` layout parameters to prevent "linear explosions" in large graphs.

*   **🧭 Precision Navigation:**
    *   **Fixed Camera Logic:** Searching for a node now correctly calculates its visual coordinates (even if off-screen) and smoothly flies the camera to the target in both 2D and 3D modes.
    *   **Source Detective:** Fixed an issue where context sources appeared as "Unknown". The system now robustly resolves file names from LightRAG's internal metadata.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
*   Fixed `GL_INVALID_FRAMEBUFFER` crashes on some systems by optimizing the renderer initialization.
*   Fixed parsing issues with certain GraphML attributes.
*   Restored the **Edit Node** functionality in the details panel that was temporarily lost in v1.1.0 refactoring.

> **Update Note:** Please **restart Obsidian** after updating to ensure the new automatic path configuration takes effect.
releases.name: v1.1.2 - Portable Brain & Visual Harmony

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This release focuses on refining the **Native Graph Manager** introduced in v1.1.0, resolving rendering glitches, and improving the overall user experience when interacting with large knowledge graphs.

### 🌟 Key Improvements

*   **⚡ High-Performance 2D Rendering:** Switched the 2D engine logic to **Sigma.js** with `fcose` layout. This significantly improves frame rates and responsiveness for vaults with thousands of nodes compared to the previous implementation.
*   **🧭 Precision Navigation:** Fixed the "Camera Travel" logic. Searching for a node or clicking on the sidebar list now accurately zooms and centers the view on the target entity in both 2D and 3D modes.
*   **🛠️ Enhanced Node Management:**
    *   **Merge Logic:** Improved the Merge modal to clearly allow selecting the "Survivor" node. Added visual feedback during processing.
    *   **Edit Node:** The details panel now allows editing not just the name, but also the **Entity Type** and **Description**, giving you full curatorial control over your graph.
*   **📄 Source Resolution:** Fixed an issue where source files were displayed as "Unknown" or raw chunk IDs. The panel now correctly resolves and links to the original Markdown or PDF files.
*   **👀 Visual Clarity:**
    *   Implemented "Stealth Labels": Text labels in 2D mode are now hidden by default to reduce clutter, appearing only on hover or selection.
    *   Improved high-contrast highlighting (Red/White) for selected nodes and neighbors.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

*   Fixed `GL_INVALID_FRAMEBUFFER` errors on some systems by optimizing the renderer initialization.
*   Fixed parsing issues with certain GraphML attributes.
*   Resolved TypeScript conflicts preventing the build in strict environments.

> **Update Instruction:** Please restart Obsidian after updating to ensure the new graph engines are loaded correctly.
releases.name: v1.1.1 - Graph Stability & Visual Polish

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
This release introduces a fully native graph visualization interface within Obsidian, support for local reranking models, and tools for ontology management. It also includes significant architectural refactoring for better stability and compatibility.

### 🚀 New Features

*   **Native Graph Manager:** A built-in explorer for the Knowledge Graph that operates directly within Obsidian.
    *   **Hybrid Rendering:** Toggle between **2D** (performance-focused using Sigma.js) and **3D** (visualization-focused using WebGL/ForceGraph) modes via settings.
    *   **Node Management:** New sidebar tools allow users to **Merge** duplicate entities or **Delete** irrelevant nodes directly from the graph view.
    *   **Node Details:** Clicking a node now displays a detailed panel with the entity description, type, and links to the specific source files used for generation.
    *   **Navigation:** Integrated search functionality to locate, focus, and zoom into specific concepts within the graph.

*   **Precision Reranking Support:**
    *   Integrated native support for **Jina AI** and **Cohere** reranking APIs.
    *   **Custom/Local Reranking:** Users can now configure a custom host endpoint. This allows connecting to local Python scripts or servers (e.g., running `BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3`) for high-precision retrieval without external dependencies.

*   **Custom Ontology Configuration:**
    *   **Auto-Detect:** New feature to analyze a representative folder of notes and generate a suggested list of **Entity Types** (e.g., `Project`, `Concept`, `Methodology`) tailored to the user's domain.
    *   **Manual Control:** Full control to define and enforce specific categories for graph construction.

### 🛠️ Improvements & Fixes

*   **Chat Message Editing:** Assistant responses can now be edited directly in the chat interface to correct factual errors or formatting before applying them to notes.
*   **CSS Isolation:** Complete refactoring of internal class names (migrating from `smtcmp` to `nrlcmp`) to prevent style conflicts when installed alongside similar plugins (e.g., Smart Composer).
*   **Console Hygiene:** Implemented log filtering to prevent non-critical server info messages from appearing as errors in the developer console.
*   **Settings UI:** Reorganized the settings panel into logical sections (Providers, Neural Backend, Chat Options) for better usability.

> **Note:** A restart of Obsidian is recommended after updating to ensure the new graph rendering engines initialize correctly.
releases.name: v1.1.0 - Native Graph Manager, Local Reranking & Custom Ontology

releases.author: oscampo
releases.description:
## 🧠 FIxed Release of Neural Composer

Fixed public release featuring:
*   Integration with local LightRAG server.
*   Dual mode: Chat (Local) and Vault Chat (Graph RAG).
*   Automated server management (Auto-start/Stop).
*   Dynamic configuration via Obsidian Settings.
*   Workflow for integration with reranking services (cloud or local)
releases.name: v1.0.0 - The Awakening
